reasoning: The spread between Moody's Baa corporate bond yields and 10-year Treasury yields (BAA10Y) was 1.65 as of October 24, 2025. This value represents a local support level, as it is the lowest point reached in the past month and was previously a turning point for a bounce. A statistical linear trend analysis (FRED data) suggests a P(increase) of 0.52 for the short term. While mean reversion to the 12-month average (approx. 1.70) suggests the spread is more likely to increase than decrease, seasonality from the prior year (Oct to Jan) showed a slight decline. Given the forecast horizon rule, I have set the probabilities to approach 0.5 (representing maximum uncertainty) as the dates become more distant, starting with a 1.65 base and expecting slight mean reversion toward 1.70 in the near term. No major economic events (Fed meetings, NFP) occur before the first resolution date, but subsequent dates will be affected by Nov/Dec Fed decisions and year-end market dynamics.
